---
name: critique-report-formatter
description: "Assembles findings from all MLPaperCritic audit skills into the final structured critique report and saves it to plans/ml_paper_critique_report.md. Invoked as the final step after all other audit skills have completed."
model: claude-haiku-4.5
---

# Skill: Critique Report Formatter

## Purpose
Assemble all findings collected from the audit skills into one final, well-structured Markdown critique report. Save the report to `plans/ml_paper_critique_report.md` (overwrite if it exists).

## How to Use the Template

**Read `./report_template.md` from this skill folder.** Use it as the base document — it provides the exact Markdown skeleton the report must follow, preventing structural omissions and ensuring AcademicWriter receives a consistently formatted triage document:

1. Copy the template's structure as the skeleton for `plans/ml_paper_critique_report.md`.
2. Replace every `<!-- ... -->` HTML comment placeholder with actual content.
3. Replace each `[X.N]` stub entry with real findings, following the issue entry format defined below.
4. Remove any stub entries that have no corresponding issue.
5. Fill in the Priority Summary table at the end with every issue number — this is AcademicWriter's primary triage tool.
6. Set the `<!-- DATE -->` placeholder to today's date.

---

## Report Structure

The report must use the following Markdown structure exactly:

```markdown
# ML Paper Critique Report

**Thesis:** Robot Localisation in Vineyard Environments  
**Generated by:** MLPaperCritic agent

---

## Executive Summary

<3–6 sentences: honest overview of the thesis's major strengths and the most critical areas
for improvement. Name the highest-priority concerns directly.>

---

## Section 1 — Narrative and Claims

<All 1.N findings from narrative-claims-auditor, in order>

---

## Section 2 — Paper Structure

<All 2.N findings from paper-structure-auditor, in order>

---

## Section 3 — Evidence Quality

<All 3.N findings from evidence-quality-auditor, in order>

---

## Section 4 — Figures and Tables

<All 4.N findings from figure-table-auditor, in order>

---

## Section 5 — Writing Style and Clarity

<All 5.N findings derived from writing-style-scanner output, grouped and prioritised>

---

## Section 6 — LaTeX Best Practices

<All 6.N findings derived from latex-hygiene-checker output, grouped and prioritised>

---

## Section 7 — Common Pitfalls

<All 7.N findings from common-pitfalls-auditor, in order>

---

## Priority Summary

### 🔴 HIGH — Address First
> These issues weaken core claims or mislead the reader.

| # | Summary |
|---|---------|
| X.N | <one-line description> |

### 🟡 MEDIUM
> These issues degrade clarity or credibility.

| # | Summary |
|---|---------|

### 🟢 LOW — Style and Hygiene
> Minor improvements; address after HIGH and MEDIUM are resolved.

| # | Summary |
|---|---------|
```

---

## Issue Entry Format

Every finding must use this format within its section:

```markdown
### [X.N] `PRIORITY: HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW`

**Location:** `<file path>` — *<section heading>* — near: *"<≤20-word quote>"*  
**Guideline:** <one-sentence rule violated>  
**Problem:** <2–5 sentences explaining what is wrong and why it matters>  

**Fix:**
1. <Concrete step, with suggested replacement text in backticks where helpful>
2. <Additional step if needed>
```

---

## Constraints

- Do NOT edit any `.tex`, `.bib`, or other thesis source files.
- Do NOT fabricate issues. Every finding must be grounded in specific text from the thesis.
- Do NOT fabricate citations, metrics, or claims that do not appear in the thesis.
- If a section is genuinely strong on a dimension, note it briefly — do not invent problems.
- Be thorough: minor issues should be included as LOW priority. AcademicWriter will triage.
- Be specific: vague feedback such as "improve clarity" is not acceptable. Every fix must be actionable enough for AcademicWriter to implement without further context.
- The Priority Summary table must list every issue number — it is the entry point for triage.
